title: erythrococca patula
intro: Erythrococca patula, also known as the bitter milkwood or bitter milkwood root, is a plant species in the Euphorbiaceae family that is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Erythrococca patula has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and it is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-diabetic properties. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
